stdout:
I’m checking the current branch state, the exact server/storage implementation, and the required validation path so the verdict is based on the code that’s actually in the repo now.

I found a newer fix commit beyond the last failed review, so I’m checking whether it actually closed the storage-layer containment gap and whether the full validation path is green on the current tree.

The latest storage-layer change is the key pivot: if `read_path_bytes` now owns path validation and containment, that likely resolves both the acceptance gap and the server/storage boundary complaint from prior reviews. I’m verifying that with the storage tests and the remaining route tests.

I’ve confirmed the storage boundary now owns path validation and repo-root containment. The remaining question is whether the route/test surface fully reflects that, especially for commit-pinned reads and the path-only endpoint.

I found one subtle thing worth checking: the path-only HTML test is still exercising an HTML fragment, not a full document, so it doesn’t prove injection there. I’m checking whether the broader helper/storage coverage is enough or if there’s still a meaningful gap.

The repo validation and test suite are already green; I just need the tail of the combined run to confirm clippy finished cleanly before I finalize the verdict.

I’ve got the passing test summary; I’m just pulling the end of the combined run to confirm clippy completed with no warnings before I lock the review.

The combined log is very long because the suite spins up a lot of temp git repos. I’m jumping straight to the `clippy` lines now so I can finish the review cleanly.

I can’t infer PR-only evidence from the local checkout, so I’m doing one quick GitHub lookup to see whether this issue has a matching PR description I can inspect instead of guessing.

The main functional/security criteria look satisfied now. I’m doing one last policy pass on the touched non-test code for project-specific review blockers like `unwrap()` in library code, since those rules are explicit here.

I found a lot of historical `unwrap()` hits elsewhere, but I only care whether this change added new ones in non-test code. I’m narrowing that to the server routes file before its `#[cfg(test)]` block.

I caught one more acceptance-detail check: the original issue explicitly asked for a test that allows filenames like `foo..bar`. I’m verifying whether that test survived the move from route-local validation into storage.

## Acceptance criteria

**Fail.** The implementation now covers the core behavior in code: `/api/raw/*path` exists and delegates commit-aware reads (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:31-42, 654-691`), all raw HTML handlers inject `<base href>` (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:547-585, 601-639, 654-691`), and repo-root / symlink containment is enforced at the storage boundary (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:761-886`; `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:381-392`).  

The remaining miss is test coverage for the acceptance item that explicitly requires allowing filenames containing `..` as part of a segment, e.g. `foo..bar`. The only live reference to that case is the validator comment (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:859-860`); the actual path-validation tests only cover rejected inputs (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:1882-1888`), and I did not find a positive `foo..bar` test in the server tests either (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:2014-2559`).

## Architecture and separation of concerns

**Pass.** The earlier route-local filesystem checks were moved into storage, which is the right boundary. Route handlers now just orchestrate HTTP concerns and delegate reads (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:554-556, 607-609, 661-663`), `CommandExecutor::read_document_bytes` passes the stored repo-relative path through instead of pre-resolving an absolute path (`crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:381-392`), and `JsonFileStorage::read_path_bytes` owns validation + containment (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:768-849, 852-885`).

## Functional paradigm

**Pass.** The new logic is mostly small pure helpers (`percent_encode_segment`, `compute_base_href`, `html_has_base_element`, `inject_base_href`) plus thin route orchestration (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:341-492, 547-691`). I did not find a boundary or style regression here.

## Correctness and invariants

**Pass.** The repo-root containment invariant is now enforced uniformly for working-tree reads via canonicalization plus `starts_with` checking (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:811-847`), and invalid repo-relative inputs are rejected before any I/O (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:852-885`). The raw handlers all inject base tags only for `text/html` and leave other content byte-faithful (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:567-573, 620-626, 673-679`).

## Safety

**Pass.** I did not find any new `unsafe` code, and this change does not touch PID-handling paths.

## Testing

**Fail.** The required validation path is green: `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed with label warnings only (`/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777068981334-ihytzm.txt:1-3`), the workspace test run passed (`/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777068981334-ihytzm.txt:787-794`), and the combined command completed cleanly after clippy (`/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777068981334-ihytzm.txt:2813-2815`).  

But the issue-specific coverage is still incomplete because the required positive `foo..bar` path case is not tested. Current storage tests only assert rejection for `../etc/passwd`, `a/../b`, `foo/..`, and `a/../../b` (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:1882-1888`), while the symlink and HTML helper coverage is otherwise solid (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:1917-1941`; `crates/server/src/routes.rs:2016-2118, 2390-2559`).

## Error handling

**Pass.** The storage layer uses typed `PathReadError` variants with contextual messages (`crates/jit/src/storage/path_errors.rs:21-41`), and the server maps those variants to HTTP status codes without string matching (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:272-280`).

## CLI contract

**Pass.** No CLI surface changed.

## Documentation

**Pass.** The touched public storage/document APIs retain doc comments and examples (`crates/jit/src/storage/mod.rs:290-319`; `crates/jit/src/storage/path_errors.rs:5-42`; `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:340-365`).

## Prior review feedback

**Pass.** The previously reported blockers are addressed in the current tree:
- repo-root containment now lives in storage instead of route code (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:768-849, 852-885`);
- issue-scoped reads no longer pre-resolve absolute paths (`crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:381-392`);
- commit-pinned HTML now takes the same base-injection path as working-tree HTML (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:567-570, 620-623, 673-676`);
- symlink escapes are covered on wildcard, issue-scoped, and path-only raw endpoints (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:2390-2559`).

## JIT issue management

**Pass.** `issue.dependencies` is empty in the provided issue context, so there is no upstream work to verify. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed, and its reported warnings are label warnings, which the review instructions explicitly allow (`/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777068981334-ihytzm.txt:1-3`).

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
